title: scrophularia libanotica subsp. armena
intro: Scrophularia libanotica subsp. armena, also known as the mountain figwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). Traditionally, the mountain figwort has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which are believed to aid in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of mountain figwort include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Mountain figwort can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, allowing individuals to harness its therapeutic properties in a variety of herbal preparations.
